title: Chamaecrista subdecrescens
taxonomy: Chamaecrista subdecrescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Chamaecrista, which is a group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Chamaecrista subdecrescens is further classified within the subgenus Chamaecrista, and it is often found growing in sandy soils. The species is native to the Americas, and its classification is widely accepted within botanical circles. Chamaecrista subdecrescens is also known by its synonym Cassia subdecrescens, highlighting its connection to the larger Cassia genus within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Chamaecrista subdecrescens, also known as a species of partridge pea, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from this plant may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, potentially contributing to its use in treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make teas and infusions for their potential health benefits. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's potential therapeutic applications.

side_effects: Chamaecrista subdecrescens is a plant species that, like other members of the Fabaceae family, may contain toxic compounds such as phytohemagglutinins and lectins.
